title: How do I embed a Monday board on my own website?
excerpt:
How did you guys do this? 

Is this feature available for users? The ability to show a board on our own websites?

posts.author: Jaewoo (Jaewoo Kim)
posts.cookedHtml:
How did you guys do this?

monday.com

monday.com Work OS: the visual platform that manages everything

monday.com is so visual and intuitive, you don’t need training. Create your ideal workflow in minutes or choose from 200+ ready-made templates to get started instantly!

Is this feature available for users? The ability to show a board on our own websites?

posts.author: Jaewoo (Jaewoo Kim)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thanks that worked for me!

Q: What does the thumbs up that shows up on hover of each pulse do?
Q: Is it not possible to view the updates/info boxes on a broadcast view?

posts.author: Krishele (Krishele Guzman)
posts.cookedHtml: I don’t actually use Broadcast View myself so can’t help you there unfortunately. If you do a search in the community for Broadcast, I know there’s been loads of other topics about Broadcast View. Maybe you’ll find your answer there or a post you could ask the question on with people more familiar with the feature.

posts.author: Lea-monday.com (Léa )
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Jaewoo,
Could you let me know which thumbs up you are referring to?
The broadcast view will display your board but you won’t be able to access the updates/info boxes from there.
In order to access them, you will have to go back to your original board.

Hope this helps
Lea

posts.author: JohnW (John Waller)
posts.cookedHtml:
@Lea-monday.com

Could you let me know which thumbs up you are referring to?

I think Jaewoo is referring to the thumbs up icon shown in the screenshot below, which only appears when hovering on an item:

posts.author: Julia-monday.com (Julia Fagelman)
posts.cookedHtml: This is currently a test we’re running which lets you “like” an item rather than a specific update in the board!
